tables.title: Sinus Tachycardia vs Supraventricular Tachycardia (SVT)
rows: Fixed, does not vary with stimulation
Clear cause: fever, pain, anxiety, dehydration, shock
Often no clear trigger, may be sudden onset
Resolves as underlying cause is treated
Does not resolve by treating a triggering illness
Supportive care; refer to PALS for rhythm-specific treatment if poorly perfusing
